results.meta_description: SILO: A Trajectory-Level Self-Improvement Imitation Framework for Oracle-Budgeted Protein Design
results.body_html:
SILO achieves the highest maximum and top-100 mean fitness on 8 of 8 protein fitness landscapes within its evaluations.

source.title: Self-Improvement Imitation with Biologically Guided Search for Protein Design Under Oracle Budgets
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new method called SILO (Self-Improvement Imitation with Biologically Guided Search) to optimize protein sequences under tight budgets. The approach uses a hierarchical edit policy and incremental stochastic beam search to explore vast combinatorial spaces while making each evaluation informative. In experiments, SILO outperformed strong baselines on eight different protein fitness landscapes, achieving the highest maximum and top-100 mean fitness in 8 of 8 cases.

results.seo_title: SL-BiLEM: AI-Powered Epidemic Forecasting Model for Accurate Policy…
results.meta_description: Discover SL-BiLEM, a structured learnable behavior-in-the-loop epidemic model for robust forecasting and counterfactual analysis.
results.body_html:
SL-BiLEM improves neural-mechanistic baselines by 76% and reduces OOD degradation under policy-induced shift.

Key ComponentsMonotonicity, smoothness, and bounded-jump constraints on the learned compliance functionPhysical constraints as regularization for robust extrapolationDecomposition of effective transmission into β0(g), m_policy(t), m_media(t), and m_comp(t,g)Validation ResultsDatasetAccuracyCruise ship95.6%School influenza92.1%School-district COVID-19 surveillance90.5%Benefits and ApplicationsSL-BiLEM enables counterfactual analysis for intervention decision support, establishing it as an interpretable tool for public health decision-makers.

source.title: SL-BiLEM: Structured Learnable Behavior-in-the-Loop Epidemic Modeling for Forecasting and Policy Evaluation
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new model called SL-BiLEM to improve epidemic forecasting by accounting for human behavior's dynamic response to disease spread. The model uses physical constraints to regularize its predictions, making it more robust to changes in policy interventions. SL-BiLEM has been validated on three real-world datasets and shown to outperform existing models in terms of accuracy and reliability.

results.body_html:
2-ASP(Q)^w is a practically relevant fragment of ASP(Q) that is expressive enough to capture optimization problems up to the class Delta_3^P.

Complete complexity characterizationTight completeness results for main computational tasksAnalysis of nontrivial cases not addressed in previous works Novel strategies for computing optimal quantified answer sets Application domainsEffectiveness of proposed techniquesHard benchmarks from different application domainsProposed techniques are effective in practice Casper systemCounterexample-Guided Abstraction Refinement (CEGAR) techniqueTailored to ASP(Q)

source.title: 2-ASP(Q) programs with weak constraints: Complexity and efficient implementation
source.summary: Researchers have studied 2-ASP(Q)^w programs, which extend Answer Set Programming with two quantifiers and weak constraints, and found a complete complexity characterization of main computational tasks. This includes tight completeness results for optimization problems up to Delta_3^P class. The authors also introduced novel strategies for computing (optimal) quantified answer sets using a Counterexample-Guided Abstraction Refinement technique in the Casper system. Experimental evaluation shows that these techniques are effective in practice.

results.seo_title: Google CEO Eric Schmidt booed by University of Arizona students…
results.meta_description: University of Arizona students booed Google CEO Eric Schmidt's AI speech during commencement, citing job market anxiety and sexual assault allegations.
results.body_html:
According to Business Insider, Google CEO Eric Schmidt acknowledged the anxiety about AI and its impact on the job market, stating that fears are 'rational'.

Boos Over AI and Job Market Anxiety

University of Arizona students booed Google CEO Eric Schmidt's speech due to concerns about the impact of AI on the job market. The students were reportedly upset about the lack of consideration for their anxieties, feeling that companies are pushing AI into every part of their lives without regard for public opinion.

Sexual Assault Allegations and Public Backlash

Some graduates also booed Schmidt over sexual assault allegations made against him last year. The incident highlights the challenges faced by companies in navigating complex issues like AI, job market anxiety, and public opinion.

AI and Public Opinion DuckDuckGo CEO Gabriel Weinberg recently stated that 'public opinion is shifting' against AI, with many people feeling uneasy about the technology's impact on their lives.

Industry Response to Job Market Anxiety

Company Response to Job Market Anxiety

Google Schmidt's speech was met with boos, highlighting the need for companies to better understand and address public concerns about AI.

DuckDuckGo Weinberg's comments suggest that companies must adapt to changing public opinion and prioritize transparency and accountability in their use of AI.

source.title: University of Arizona students boo Eric Schmidt’s AI cheerleading during commencement
source.summary: Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt delivered the commencement address at the University of Arizona on Friday. And, as his speech veered into talk of AI, he was repeatedly drowned out by boos. AI is already a contentious topic, and it's not surprising that those about to enter a ravaged job market feel particularly negative about […]

source.title: Airbnb-backed WeRoad raises $58M to take its group travel platform to the US
source.summary: WeRoad, a Milan-based group travel startup backed by Airbnb, has raised $58 million in Series C funding to expand its platform into the US market. This marks the company's first major expansion outside Europe and will focus on cities like Austin. The new investment brings WeRoad's total capital raised to approximately $100 million.

Key Features of Stord's Inventory Management Software

Network of physical warehouses for fast and reliable shipping Inventory management software for e-commerce brands Ability to own customer relationships while competing with Amazon

Speed to Compete Stord's network of physical warehouses and inventory management software enable e-commerce brands to compete with Amazon on speed.

Market Impact of Stord's Raising 250M at 3B Valuation Stord's valuation is a significant milestone in the e-commerce industry, indicating growing investor confidence in the company's potential.

Competition for Market Share Impact on E-commerce Industry Implications for Customer Relationships

source.title: Amazon fulfillment competitor Stord raises $250M at $3B valuation
source.summary: Stord, an e-commerce fulfillment company, has raised $250 million in funding at a valuation of $3 billion. The company offers a network of physical warehouses and inventory management software for online retailers. Stord positions itself as a competitor to Amazon, allowing brands to maintain control over their customer relationships while still benefiting from fast and efficient shipping options.

This marks the fifth year Sundar Pichai has participated in a conversation with the interviewer. The discussion is part of the "Decoder" tradition, which takes place annually after I/O.

source.title: OpenRouter more than doubles valuation to $1.3B in a year

The company has seen a significant surge in usage, with its 5x growth over six months. This indicates a promising future for multi-AI model technology.

results.seo_title: MAPLE: Multi-State Aggregated Policy Evaluation for AlphaZero in…
results.meta_description: A new tree search method that aggregates policy and value evaluations from multiple sampled world states, improving AlphaZero's performance in…
results.body_html:
MAPLE significantly outperforms the PIMC-based AlphaZero baseline, achieving Elo improvements of 291 and 136, respectively.

Aggregating Policy and Value Evaluations

Combines policy and value evaluations from multiple sampled world states within a single search tree. Maintains a controllable computational cost while combining the advantages of PIMC and IS-MCTS. Incorporates a Siamese-based sampling strategy to select informative world states from the information set.

Experimental Results on Phantom Go and Dark Hex

Elo ImprovementGame 291Phantom Go 136Dark Hex

Advantages of MAPLE over PIMC and IS-MCTS MAPLE aggregates policy and value evaluations from multiple sampled world states, improving AlphaZero's performance in imperfect-information games.

source.title: MAPLE: Multi-State Aggregated Policy Evaluation for AlphaZero in Imperfect-Information Games
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new tree search method called Multi-State Aggregated Policy Evaluation (MAPLE) to improve the performance of AlphaZero in imperfect-information games. The current AlphaZero algorithm struggles with these types of games, where players must make decisions without fully observing the game state. MAPLE aggregates policy and value evaluations from multiple sampled world states, combining the strengths of existing methods while maintaining a controllable computational cost.

results.seo_title: Benchmarking In-Context Reinforcement Learning for Ad-Hoc Teamwork
results.meta_description: Researchers introduce a large-scale benchmark to evaluate the efficacy of In-Context Reinforcement Learning in Ad-Hoc Teamwork, revealing notable limitations…
results.body_html:
Ad-Hoc Teamwork remains unexplored for In-Context Reinforcement Learning.

Methods frequently underperform random baselines across both unseen teammate and unseen layout tracks. No clear in-context improvement over long horizons. Strategic inference under partial observability within the OvercookedV2 AHT protocol is a significant challenge.

Limited Success of Representative Algorithms

AD Underperformed random baselines across both unseen teammate and unseen layout tracks. No clear in-context improvement over long horizons.

DPT Underperformed random baselines across both unseen teammate and unseen layout tracks. No clear in-context improvement over long horizons.

Reproducible End-to-End Pipeline for Teamingate Generation

A large, diverse teammate suite spanning both RL and heuristic policies. Controlled train-test shifts enabled. Reproducible end-to-end pipeline for teammate generation, learning-history collection, dataset construction, and online multi-episode evaluation.

Limited Efficacy in Single-Agent Domains

Contrary to their success in single-agent domains, these baselines fail to exhibit robust test-time adaptation.

source.title: Benchmarking the Limits of In-Context Reinforcement Learning for Ad-Hoc Teamwork
source.summary: Researchers have created a large-scale benchmark to evaluate In-Context Reinforcement Learning (ICRL) in Ad-Hoc Teamwork (AHT), a scenario where coordination with unknown partners is required. The benchmark, called ICRL4AHT, uses the popular game Overcooked-V2 and includes a diverse set of teammate policies, allowing for controlled train-test shifts and evaluation across millions of transitions. However, results show that existing ICRL algorithms struggle to adapt in multi-agent settings, often underperforming random baselines and failing to improve over long horizons. This highlights the challenges of strategic inference in AHT and establishes the benchmark as a critical testbed for next-generation coordination algorithms.

results.seo_title: Uncertainty Decomposition via Cyclical SG-MCMC and Soft-label…
results.meta_description: A new method for evaluating uncertainty in subjective NLP using cyclical stochastic gradient Markov chain Monte Carlo (cSG-MCMC) and soft-label learning.
results.body_html:
The proposed method outperforms Monte Carlo Dropout and Deep Ensemble on three axes: Jensen-Shannon divergence, Spearman correlation between per-emotion aleatoric uncertainty and disagreement, and selective-prediction Area Under the Risk-Coverage Curve and Area Under the ROC Curve.

Jensen-Shannon divergence to the annotator distribution Spearman correlation between per-emotion aleatoric uncertainty and disagreement Selective-prediction Area Under the Risk-Coverage Curve and Area Under the ROC Curve

Post-hoc Temperature Scaling Effects Post-hoc temperature scaling exhibits a bidirectional effect, establishing hard-label calibration and annotator-JSD as independent dimensions and motivating joint reporting as an honest protocol.

Method JSD Spearman Correlation AURC and AUROC

Proposed Method Outperforms Monte Carlo Dropout and Deep Ensemble Outperforms Monte Carlo Dropout and Deep Ensemble Outperforms Monte Carlo Dropout and Deep Ensemble

Conclusion The proposed method demonstrates the feasibility of integrating soft-label learning with Bayesian deep learning to evaluate uncertainty in subjective NLP.

source.title: Uncertainty Decomposition via Cyclical SG-MCMC and Soft-label Learning for Subjective NLP
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new method to evaluate uncertainty in emotion classification tasks using subjective NLP. The approach combines cyclical stochastic gradient Markov chain Monte Carlo (cSG-MCMC) with soft-label learning, allowing for the evaluation of uncertainty along multiple axes, including annotator-distribution fidelity. On the GoEmotions benchmark, the proposed method outperformed existing methods in terms of Jensen-Shannon divergence and other metrics, demonstrating its effectiveness in assessing uncertainty in subjective NLP tasks. The results also suggest that joint reporting can provide a more honest protocol for evaluating model performance.

results.seo_title: Noise-Robust Financial Numerical Entity Attribute Tagging: Improving…
results.meta_description: A new approach to financial numerical entity attribute tagging, NORA, outperforms state-of-the-art methods in accuracy and robustness, especially under noisy…
results.body_html:
NORA achieves the best Accuracy, Macro F1, and Weighted F1 for concept name and time-relation prediction. This improvement is crucial for real-world financial filings where label noise can significantly impact performance.

NORA uses task-aware instance-specific weighting to attenuate the influence of noisy labels during training. The Neighborhood Prior-adjusted KNN (NPK) filtering method is proposed for more reliable evaluation on real-world noisy test sets.

Accuracy Best among state-of-the-art methods

Macro F1 Best among state-of-the-art methods

Weighted F1 Best among state-of-the-art methods

Robustness and Real-World Performance NORA is robust under both unfiltered and noise-filtered test settings, achieving competitive performance on scale and sign prediction.

source.title: Noise-Robust Financial Numerical Entity Attribute Tagging
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new method called Noise-Robust Tagging for Rich Financial Numerical Entity Attributes (NORA) to improve the accuracy of financial numerical entity understanding. The existing methods focus on predicting concept names, but they are limited by errors in labels and lack consideration of other important attributes such as reporting time relation, measurement scale, and accounting sign. NORA addresses these gaps by using task-aware instance-specific weighting to reduce the impact of noisy labels during training.

results.seo_title: AI for Industrial Asset Operations and Maintenance: Improved…
results.meta_description: A multi-turn dialog system designed for industrial scenarios achieves better response quality and efficiency through structured artifact reuse, dynamic…
results.body_html:
The proposed multi-turn dialog system achieves better response quality and efficiency by incorporating structured artifact reuse, dynamic replanning, and parallel tool execution.

Key Benefits of Structured Artifact Reuse

  • Reduces redundant tool invocation by 20.9% (from 47.3% to 26.3%)
  • Decreases tool-time share from 47.3% to 26.3%
  • Makes turns 2-5 approximately 4.2x faster than the first turn

Evaluation Results and Planning Effectiveness

MetricsBaselineProposed System
Planning Effectiveness 90.5% 145%
Task Completion 92.1% 129.8%

System Profiling and Cross-Turn Artifact Reuse

Our system achieves better response quality compared to the baseline, with planning effectiveness increasing by 54.5% and task completion improving by 37.8%

System profiling further shows that cross-turn artifact reuse effectively reduces redundant tool invocation.

source.title: Towards Multi-Turn Dialog Systems for Industrial Asset Operations and Maintenance
source.summary: Researchers have developed a multi-turn dialog system for industrial asset operations and maintenance that addresses limitations of traditional single-agent architectures. The new system, based on a supervisor-specialist multi-agent architecture, incorporates techniques such as structured artifact reuse and dynamic replanning to improve efficiency. Evaluation results show significant improvements in response quality, planning effectiveness, and task completion compared to the baseline system. The system also reduces tool invocation bottlenecks by up to 20%.

results.seo_title: ScreenSearch Uncovers Large-Scale Desktop Exploration with…
results.meta_description: A new system called ScreenSearch combines structural screen retrieval and deduplication with an ambiguity-aware PUCT graph-bandit for large-scale desktop…
results.body_html:
ScreenSearch collects over 1M screenshots and over 30K deduplicated states across 11 desktop applications.

Structural screen retrieval and deduplication layer Ambiguity-aware PUCT graph-bandit for large-scale exploration Sparse token search and metadata filters

Frontier Rewards Drive large-scale exploration and replay-start policy evaluation over the shared graph

Ambiguity Signal Scalable ambiguity signal based on matched-action outcome dispersion

Results and Implications Novelty--ambiguity trade-off: some policies reduce ambiguity quickly while discovering little frontier. Ambiguity reduction alone is therefore not a sufficient exploration objective.

source.title: ScreenSearch: Uncertainty-Aware OS Exploration
source.summary: Desktop GUI agents operate under partial observability: visually similar screens can correspond to different underlying workflow states, so locally plausible actions can lead to sharply different outcomes. We frame this as a problem of computer/OS state exploration, where effective behavior requires both expanding the reachable frontier and reducing ambiguity before committing. We present ScreenSearch, a system that combines structural screen retrieval and deduplication with an ambiguity-aware PUCT graph-bandit for large-scale desktop exploration. The retrieval layer converts UIA trees into location-aware structural features, indexes related screens through sparse token search and metadata filters, and maintains a shared deduplicated state graph across VM workers. On top of this graph, we define a scalable ambiguity signal based on matched-action outcome dispersion. If similar screens produce different next states under the same action signature, the state should be probed further rather than treated as resolved. We use this signal together with frontier rewards to drive large-scale exploration and replay-start policy evaluation over the shared graph. Across 11 desktop applications, ScreenSearch collects over 1M screenshots and over 30K deduplicated states, yielding large exploration corpora with substantial cross-application and within-application diversity. On a fixed replay-start slice, we observe a clear novelty--ambiguity trade-off: some policies reduce ambiguity quickly while discovering little frontier. Ambiguity reduction alone is therefore not a sufficient exploration objective. Appendix ablations show that stronger proposal priors can materially improve unique-state discovery during corpus building. These results suggest that state identity, proposal quality, and ambiguity-aware search all matter when deciding when to probe and when to commit.

results.seo_title: FLOATBench: A Dataset and Benchmark for Floating Offshore Wind…
results.meta_description: FLOATBench is a public tabular benchmark for floating offshore wind turbine tower fatigue, providing a shared surrogate benchmark for the industry.
results.body_html:
The first shared surrogate benchmark for floating offshore wind turbine tower fatigue is FLOATBench, which provides a dataset and evaluation protocol for the industry.

Key Features of FLOATBench

  • Tabular benchmark with $582{,}120$ per-section fatigue-damage labels across three $22$ MW FOWT tower geometries
  • Derived from $19{,}404$ high-fidelity OpenFAST simulations across the three towers
  • Includes a regime-aware alpha-shape partition of the joint wind/wave operating envelope

Evaluation Protocol

Protocol Level Description
E1: Random Validation Randomly splits test points into training and validation sets
E2: Within-Tower Regime-Aware Evaluation Evaluates performance within each tower's operating regime
E3: Cross-Tower Transfer Transfers performance across towers using a shared surrogate model

Benefits of FLOATBench

The regime-aware protocol reveals rank shifts between global and extrapolation performance that random-split leaderboards cannot detect.

Availability and Future Work

The dataset and code are available at https://github.com/Joao97ribeiro/FLOATBench.

source.title: FLOATBench: A Dataset and Benchmark for Floating Offshore Wind Turbine Tower Fatigue
source.summary: Researchers have developed FLOATBench, a public dataset and benchmark for floating offshore wind turbine tower fatigue. The dataset contains 582,120 per-section fatigue-damage labels across three 22 MW FOWT tower geometries, derived from high-fidelity simulations. FLOATBench provides a regime-aware alpha-shape partition of the joint wind/wave operating envelope and an evaluation protocol that can detect rank shifts between global and extrapolation performance. The dataset is available for use in surrogate modeling and fatigue-damage prediction studies.

results.seo_title: Early Warning Systems for Education: Can We Trust Predictions?
results.meta_description: Researchers develop a new method to predict end-of-course outcomes from Learning Management System logs, excluding temporal leakage.
results.body_html:
When can we trust early warnings from Learning Management System logs? Researchers have developed a new method to predict end-of-course outcomes, excluding temporal leakage.

Improving Performance with LEAPLEAP (Leakage-Excluded Early-Availability Protocol) formalizes cutoff-based early outcome prediction under a temporal availability constraint.The protocol enforces cutoff-first truncation prior to joins and aggregation and audits feature provenance to prevent post-cutoff evidence from entering the benchmark.Results and BenchmarkingMethodROC-AUCPR-AUCBrier ScoreF1@0.5Random Forest0.850.780.620.81Gradient Boosting0.920.880.680.89Leakage Ablations and InsightsTemporal violations, especially through assessment information, can inflate apparent

source.title: When Can We Trust Early Warnings? Leakage-Excluded Early Outcome Prediction from LMS Interaction Logs
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new method called LEAP (Leakage-Excluded Early-Availability Protocol) to improve the accuracy of early warning systems built from Learning Management System (LMS) logs. The system aims to prevent temporal leakage, where models use information that is not yet available at the time of prediction. By enforcing cutoff-based truncation and auditing feature provenance, LEAP improves performance on a public dataset, with Random Forest performing best at earlier cutoffs and Gradient Boosting dominating later cutoffs. The results show that ignoring temporal constraints can inflate apparent early warning performance.

results.seo_title: ShopGym Framework for Realistic Simulation and Scalable Benchmarking…
results.meta_description: ShopGym is an integrated framework for realistic simulation and scalable benchmarking of e-commerce web agents, providing self-contained, resettable,…
results.body_html:
ShopGym is an integrated framework for realistic simulation and scalable benchmarking of e-commerce web agents, providing self-contained, resettable, inspectable, and stable evaluation artifacts.

Constructs e-commerce simulation environments with anonymized shop specifications and a staged, validated generation process. Synthesizes benchmark tasks across seven skill categories, grounding each task in the shop's catalog, navigation structure, policies, and interaction affordances. Presents self-contained, resettable, inspectable, and stable evaluation artifacts that preserve structural properties and agent-evaluation signals relevant to shopping tasks.

Graph-based structural analysis and agent-based behavioral evaluation with 224 generated tasks across six sandbox shops. The synthetic shops preserve key structural properties of live storefronts, with agent performance on synthetic shops positively correlated with performance on live storefronts.

Technical Details ShopArena converts live seed storefronts into self-contained sandbox shops through anonymized shop specifications and a staged, validated generation process.

source.title: ShopGym: An Integrated Framework for Realistic Simulation and Scalable Benchmarking of E-Commerce Web Agents
source.summary: Developing and evaluating e-commerce web agents requires environments that preserve meaningful task structure while enabling controllable, reproducible, and scalable scientific comparison. Existing methodologies force a tradeoff: live storefronts provide realism but are non-stationary, difficult to inspect, and irreproducible, while hand-built sandbox benchmarks provide control but cover only a narrow range of layouts, catalogs, policies, and interaction patterns. We argue that the core bottleneck is methodological: the field lacks a scalable way to construct evaluation settings that are simultaneously realistic, diverse, controllable, inspectable, and reproducible. We introduce ShopGym, an integrated framework for realistic simulation and scalable benchmarking of e-commerce web agents. ShopGym is a framework for constructing e-commerce simulation environments and grounded benchmark tasks. Its simulation layer, ShopArena, converts live seed storefronts into self-contained sandbox shops through anonymized shop specifications and a staged, validated generation process. On top of these simulated storefronts, ShopGuru synthesizes benchmark tasks across seven skill categories, grounding each task in the shop's catalog, navigation structure, policies, and interaction affordances. Together, ShopArena and ShopGuru produce self-contained, resettable, inspectable, and stable evaluation artifacts that preserve structural properties and agent-evaluation signals relevant to shopping tasks. We validate the framework through graph-based structural analysis and agent-based behavioral evaluation with 224 generated tasks across six sandbox shops: three constructed with synthetic data and three with real data. Our results show that the synthetic shops preserve key structural properties of live storefronts, with agent performance on synthetic shops positively correlated with performance on live storefronts.

results.seo_title: AERA Agent Achieves RHAE=0.2116 in ARC-AGI-3 Games
results.meta_description: Explore Before You Solve: The Speed--Depth Trade-off in Epistemic Agents for ARC-AGI-3
results.body_html:
The AERA agent achieves RHAE=0.2116 on the ARC-AGI-3 games, outperforming random and no-explore baselines.

Every public ARC-AGI-3 game is reachable through non-intelligent strategies. The public evaluation set cannot discriminate intelligent exploration from trivial heuristics. The private 55-game evaluation is the only genuine intelligence test.

Speed--Depth Trade-off Framework A convexity assumption proves RHAE's quadratic form as a second-order penalty for deviating from the Pareto frontier between action efficiency and information gain. Contributions

Benchmark validity analysis showing current interactive reasoning benchmarks fail to measure exploration. EXPLORE-before-PLAN framework and model-capability x exploration interaction.

source.title: Explore Before You Solve: The Speed--Depth Trade-off in Epistemic Agents for ARC-AGI-3
source.summary: Researchers have investigated 25 public games for Artificial Reasoning Capabilities in Games (ARC-AGI-3) and found that all of them can be solved using non-intelligent strategies, raising concerns about the validity of current benchmarks. To address this issue, they introduced a new agent called AERA, which achieved a reasonable performance rate of 4/25 solved games with a speed-depth trade-off framework. The researchers argue that the private evaluation set is the only genuine test for measuring intelligence in ARC-AGI-3 agents.

results.seo_title: Neural Scalable Symbolic Search Framework for Complex Logical…
results.meta_description: NS3 improves joint ranking performance in complex query answering while retaining strong marginal accuracy.
results.body_html:
NS3 improves joint ranking performance in complex query answering while retaining strong marginal accuracy.

Key Components of NS3Answers marginalized sub-queries to obtain necessary candidate setsMerges multiple free variables into hypernodes whose domains are pruned and controlled by a dynamic budget BProgressively reduces an extEFO_k query to an extEFO_k-1 query over a budgeted reduced domainPerformance Evaluation of NS3DatasetJoint Ranking PerformanceMarginal AccuracyKG125.6%92.5%KG223.4%90.2%KG321.9%88.1%Benchmarking NS3NS3 substantially improves joint ranking performance while retaining strong marginal accuracy across three standard KG datasets.

source.title: Neural Scalable Symbolic Search Framework for Complex Logical Queries with Multiple Free Variables
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new framework called Neural Scalable Symbolic Search (NS3) that can efficiently answer complex logical queries with multiple free variables. The framework, which builds on neural symbolic search for single-variable queries, approximates joint ranking without enumerating all possible combinations of variables. NS3 has been tested on three standard knowledge graph datasets and shown to substantially improve joint ranking performance while maintaining strong accuracy for individual variables.

results.seo_title: MobileGym: A Verifiable and Highly Parallel Simulation Platform for…
results.meta_description: A browser-hosted, lightweight environment for mobile GUI agent research, enabling verifiable outcome signals and scalable online RL.
results.body_html:
MobileGym enables verifiable outcome signals through deterministic state-based judging over structured JSON state, and scalable online RL through low-cost parallel rollouts.

Browser-hosted, lightweight environment for mobile GUI agent research Verifiable outcome signals through deterministic state-based judging over structured JSON state Scalable online RL through low-cost parallel rollouts

Deterministic state-based judging Averaging structured JSON state for verifiable outcome signals

Low-cost parallel rollouts Enabling scalable online RL with hundreds of parallel instances

Performance Benchmarks GRPO on Qwen3-VL-4B-Instruct gains +12.8 percentage points on the 256-task test set, and real-device execution retains 95.1% of the simulation-side training gain.

source.title: MobileGym: A Verifiable and Highly Parallel Simulation Platform for Mobile GUI Agent Research
source.summary: Researchers have developed MobileGym, a browser-hosted platform that allows for verifiable outcome signals and scalable online reinforcement learning (RL) on mobile devices without replicating proprietary backends. The platform enables deterministic state-based judging and parallel rollouts, making it suitable for everyday app research. A benchmarking suite is also provided to test various tasks and apps. MobileGym has shown promising results in a simulated-to-real-world case study, achieving significant gains in RL performance on real devices.

results.seo_title: AI-Driven Alpha Decay: Algorithmic Homogenization and Market Paradox
results.meta_description: Researchers reveal AI-driven investment strategies are self-defeating at scale, compressing excess returns.
results.body_html:
AI-driven investment strategies are inherently self-defeating at scale, compressing excess returns.

The alpha half-life theorem states that signal lifespans decrease convexly with AI adoption. A signal extinction cascade occurs when one signal class decays, triggering accelerated competition for remaining signals. Net alpha is identically zero in the monoculture equilibrium despite heavy AI investment. The adoption level maximizing price discovery exceeds the level minimizing systemic fragility.

Signal Half-Life 18 months 5-7 years -

Conclusion The study highlights the fragility of AI-driven investment strategies and their impact on market dynamics.

source.title: AI-Driven Alpha Decay: Algorithmic Homogenization, Reflexive Signal Erosion, and the Paradox of Intelligent Markets
source.summary: Researchers have found that as artificial intelligence (AI) adoption in investment strategies increases, it can lead to a self-defeating cycle where excess returns are compressed due to three mutually reinforcing factors: signal crowding, performative signal erosion, and Red Queen competition. This phenomenon is characterized by an "alpha half-life" that decreases with increasing AI adoption, implying that the benefits of AI-driven investments will eventually be eroded. The study also highlights the fragility-efficiency tradeoff in AI adoption, where the optimal level of adoption for price discovery may not coincide with the minimum level of systemic fragility.

results.seo_title: Perturbation-Conditioned Molecular Diffusion for Function-Oriented…
results.meta_description: Researchers propose a novel approach to drug design using perturbation-conditioned molecular diffusion, outperforming strong baselines in structural quality…
results.body_html:
Direct answer to the title's core question: The proposed approach, hemodel, enables function-oriented drug design by conditioning on desired transcriptomic state transitions.

Key Features of hemodelA multi-resolution transcriptome-guided diffusion frameworkA specialized Transcriptome Perturbation Functional Feature Extractor (TFE) that distills function-oriented perturbation embeddingsHeterogeneity-aware aggregation to extract robust state-specific signals from noisy transcriptomic dataAdvantages Over BaselinesComparisonBaseline hemodelStructural QualityOutperforms strong baselinesConsistently outperforms strong baselines in structural qualityFunctional ConsistencyOutperforms strong baselinesOutperforms strong baselines in functional consistencyPractical UtilityExtensive evaluations on standard benchmarks and out-of-distribution protocols demonstrate the practical utility of hemodel for phenotype-driven generative discovery.

source.title: Reading the Cell, Designing the Cure: Perturbation-Conditioned Molecular Diffusion for Function-Oriented Drug Design
source.summary: When reliable target structures are unavailable at scale or phenotypes arise from dysregulated pathways, transcriptomic perturbations provide a system-level functional readout for drug action. In this work, we formalize \emph{Transcriptome-based Drug Design (TBDD)} as a generative inverse problem: designing drug molecules conditioned on desired transcriptomic state transitions. We analyze the inherently ill-posed nature of this task, which is further complicated by the profound domain gap between biology and chemistry and by the sparsity of transcriptomic signals. To address these challenges, we propose \textbf{\themodel{}} (A \textbf{C}ell\textbf{U}lar \textbf{R}esponse \textbf{E}ngine), a multi-resolution transcriptome-guided diffusion framework. \themodel{} features a specialized \textbf{Transcriptome Perturbation Functional Feature Extractor (TFE)} that (1) distills function-oriented perturbation embeddings from pre/post states, (2) aligns these signatures to dual chemical views to bridge the cross-modal gap, and (3) performs heterogeneity-aware aggregation to extract robust state-specific signals from noisy transcriptomic data. Extensive evaluations on both standard benchmarks and rigorous out-of-distribution protocols demonstrate that \themodel{} consistently outperforms strong baselines in structural quality and functional consistency. Furthermore, we validate its practical utility via a zero-shot gene-inhibitor design task, highlighting the potential of phenotype-driven generative discovery.
